profile.name: Michael Aiello
profile.headline: Full-stack engineer — operational web apps, GIS, SQL Server, and edge AI
profile.bio: I develop web applications that support real operations—healthcare IT field workflows, utility and telephony informatics, facility asset mapping, CMS-style content tools, and edge AI on Jetson hardware. Most repositories here are deliberate POCs and learning sandboxes: they demonstrate how I structure full-stack work, integrate alongside real job responsibilities when possible, and ramp quickly on domain-specific tools. I treat enterprise and client systems with respect for standards, security, and integration paths I would learn on the job rather than guess in a public repo.
philosophy: POC-first repos that prove aptitude without pretending to replace an organization's production standards
Operational context over toy demos—healthcare deployment prep, call analytics, floor-plan asset tracking, utility GIS shells
Fast, structured learning—reference codebases (SSMS explorer, CMS basics) I can extend when a real scenario appears
Honest phase labels—what runs today, what was side-tested vs officially integrated, and what would come next with proper access
profile.contactNote: Reach out via GitHub profile or issues on this repository.

projects.title: DOT Work — Multi-State GIS Platform POC
projects.description: Department of Transportation proof-of-concept: USA Mapbox landing page, per-state database routing, and New Hampshire as the first fully connected state module.
projects.inspiration: Curiosity about real-world GIS led me to DOT-based application systems and the wide variation across U.S. states. I scaffolded a small, open-ended POC to learn simple basics without assuming any one state's GIS approach—while developing in New Hampshire and exploring seasonal DOT/GIS internship opportunities.
projects.currentState: Early POC with NH as the first connected state module; national shell and per-state routing pattern in place.
projects.futureDirections: Deepen state-specific modules as domain requirements become clear; align with real DOT data and integration standards when working inside an agency context.
deployment: Mapbox tokens managed in SQL (not committed)

projects.title: SwimSwam — Production Stack
projects.description: Production deployment repository for high-school swim team management: JavaScript frontend, C# backend, and Postgres (Supabase) migrations.
projects.inspiration: As an assistant swim coach with 10+ years of competitive swimming, I applied web development skills to team management—schedules, meets, rosters, and related workflows—and hosted a live test run for the team I coached.
projects.currentState: Served its purpose in production for one season; no longer hosted. Informed real user feedback and in-production iteration experience.
projects.futureDirections: Refine and redeploy for a future coaching season or fork patterns into a next-version dev workspace.

projects.title: Aiello Roofing — Production
projects.description: Production roofing business showcase: ASP.NET Core API, React + Vite + Mapbox client, and Supabase Postgres migrations. Live at aielloroofing.online.
projects.inspiration: Built for a family member's roofing company to supplement or replace their prior site—map-based project showcase plus editor login to create location-based jobs, a capability the old website lacked. First project I hosted on the public internet (VM, Nginx, SSL) rather than only on my machine.
projects.currentState: Production-hosted client application with map UI and content management for project showcases.
projects.futureDirections: Ongoing content and feature polish as the business needs evolve.

projects.title: SwimSwam — Development
projects.description: Active development workspace for swim team management software with frontend, backend, and utility tooling.
projects.inspiration: Isolate experimentation and next-season features from the production deploy repository used during the coached team's test run.
projects.currentState: Development workspace for future iterations after the production season archive.
projects.futureDirections: Carry forward lessons from production feedback into refined team-management features.
deployment: Sync selected changes to production repo

projects.title: Water Work — Utility Geospatial Digital Twin
projects.description: Water infrastructure digital-twin POC: .NET API, SQL Server stored procedures, Mapbox static UI, spatial queries and network tracing.
projects.inspiration: Growing interest in environmental engineering and the ubiquity of water-utility operations—parallel to my electrical grid learning POC—to visualize water informatics in a GIS-style UI with related database nodes and objects.
projects.currentState: Solid architectural foundation; core flows work but the UI and polish are not finished.
projects.futureDirections: Refine operator-facing views, polish map layers, and deepen domain-specific tracing and reporting.

projects.title: Grid Work — Utility IoT Demo
projects.description: Grid IoT proof-of-concept: Blazor hybrid app, SQL Server telemetry, Mapbox operations map, alarms, and simulated device readings.
projects.inspiration: While pursuing an IT software associate role at a northeastern utilities energy company, I built a POC to understand what a grid operator's application might include—GIS UI, energy informatics, and relationships between telemetry database objects.
projects.currentState: Functional learning codebase; in retrospect skewed toward an electrical-engineering overview more than a client-facing utility operations portal.
projects.futureDirections: Reframe views toward operator and customer-facing workflows; tighten integration narratives for real utility standards.

projects.title: Mapbot — Geospatial Application
projects.description: Map-centric application with Go backend service, JavaScript frontend, and database layer for geospatial workflows.
projects.inspiration: A maturing awareness of government, law-enforcement, and military targeting systems—and a Palantir internship application—led me to scaffold a POC in Palantir-aligned languages (Go, TypeScript) to visualize operations-style geospatial workflows.
projects.currentState: Infrastructure and stack alignment in place; functionality is not fully extensive or polished.
projects.futureDirections: Expand operational workflows, data linking, and production-hardening only within appropriate enterprise contexts.

projects.title: Map Asset — Floor Plan Asset Tracking
projects.description: POC for tracking assets on uploaded floor-plan maps with hierarchical Region → State → Location → Area → MapImage → Assets.
projects.inspiration: Inspired by a floor-plan asset tool used during a healthcare IT contract—draggable icons on building images correlating device locations. I rebuilt a generic version for enterprises with varied asset types, open labels for device fields, uploadable floor plans, and toggles to show all assets or a selected subset.
projects.currentState: Works as intended for flexible facility and asset modeling in a workstation POC.
projects.futureDirections: Auth, enterprise RBAC, and connector patterns if adopted inside a specific organization.

projects.title: HHC Patient Education — Healthcare POC
projects.description: Patient education platform POC: ASP.NET Core MVC, interactive modules, quizzes, visual learning, and optional Epic/SMART integration path.
projects.inspiration: Side project alongside a healthcare IT contract—motivated by patient rights and informed consent. Envisioned quiz-style education synced with real charts and Epic data to improve the patient experience around treatments, procedures, and medications.
projects.currentState: Side-development POC with synthetic data; not tied to contract workflows or live EHR feeds.
projects.futureDirections: Epic/FHIR and chart-backed assignments—the stated next step if the concept were pursued inside a health system.

projects.title: HHC IT Field Services — Desktop POC
projects.description: Native WPF desktop for field operations: Excel import coordination grid, AD tools, and Set PC profile staging with integrity checks.
projects.inspiration: Built during a nine-month healthcare IT contract to organize laptop and asset deployment prep across regions, departments, and software profiles—replacing ad-hoc coordination with a structured desktop workflow.
projects.currentState: Ran successfully alongside real tier-2 domain workflows and collaborative scenarios; proved viability as a side test but was not officially integrated into enterprise release processes.
projects.futureDirections: Formal packaging, Authenticode signing, and IT-approved deployment paths if brought into production change control.

projects.title: Telephony Analytics Dashboard
projects.description: Telephony-agnostic call analytics POC: normalized SQL data, ASP.NET Core API, Blazor Server dashboard with Mapbox regional views.
projects.inspiration: After shipping Avaya-integrated sales call analytics in manufacturing production, I wanted a generic telephony POC—open adapter style, not Avaya-only—with GIS/map views for territory, call-area informatics, and cross-sector analytics.
projects.currentState: Working POC with mock and file-based ingestion plus regional map dashboards.
projects.futureDirections: Additional provider adapters and production-grade ingestion hardening per target enterprise.

projects.title: CMS Basics — Headless Content POC
projects.description: Generic CMS POC with separate Authoring and Delivery microservices, React SPAs, and author-editable theming.
projects.inspiration: Intentionally generic learning project to understand CMS-style interfaces—how client-facing users edit and publish web content without tying the POC to one company's product.
projects.currentState: Delivers intended authoring vs delivery separation and theme editing; suitable as a teaching/reference codebase.
projects.futureDirections: Richer content models, workflow approvals, and hosting patterns for a real tenant.
deployment: SQL Server LocalDB for authoring DB

projects.title: Aphmorism — AI-Assisted Library POC
projects.description: Local-library aphmorism POC: LLM-assisted generation grounded in leader-verified quotes, Blazor UI, and structured patron ratings.
projects.inspiration: Sparked by a library IT/database admin job application and an interest in philosophy—envisioned a supplemental tool for book clubs to rate AI-generated aphmorisms that synthesize or reconcile quotes from different authors, with humans judging quality rather than replacing live debate.
projects.currentState: Core scaffolding and data flows work; earlier phase—OpenAI key integration and live generation testing still outstanding.
projects.futureDirections: Wire LLM provider configuration, run patron rating sessions end-to-end, and polish library-leader approval workflows.
deployment: LLM credentials via admin config (not published)

projects.title: Jetson Local LLM Device
projects.description: Offline-first Jetson Orin Nano chat UI with local Ollama, FastAPI proxy, CSI camera snapshots, and multimodal vision models.
projects.inspiration: Step up from Raspberry Pi and Arduino tinkering to a Jetson dev kit for serious edge/IoT work—first app was a fully offline local LLM with camera photo analysis (half-serious survival-scenario motivation, wholly serious learning goals).
projects.currentState: Local LLM inference and photo upload/analysis work on device; companion camera bring-up repo documents hardware setup.
projects.futureDirections: RAG-style context, conversational memory, and persisted chat sessions for more context-aware dialogue.

projects.title: Jetson + e-CAM86 Camera Bring-Up
projects.description: Hardware bring-up documentation for Jetson Orin Nano with e-con e-CAM86 camera on JetPack 6.1 / L4T R36.4.0.
projects.inspiration: Prerequisite hardware path for the local LLM device—document JetPack flash, e-CAM86 drivers, and GStreamer verification separately from application code.
projects.currentState: Documented bring-up workflow; large binaries stay local under dev-resources.
projects.futureDirections: Keep version matrix current as JetPack and camera packages update.
architecture: Prerequisite repo for LLM device project
directoryOverview: dev-resources/ — local JetPack and driver archives (not in git)

projects.title: Excel SQL — Blazor Data Tooling POC
projects.description: Blazor Server POC bridging Excel workbooks and SQL Server: connectivity checks, DDL generation, scripted apply, and read-only SQL templates.
projects.inspiration: After a year or two of work experience, I noticed how often Excel and SQL coexist in business processes and wanted a simple POC to upload a sheet, map rows and columns, and visualize conversion toward SQL-friendly structures for web app development.
projects.currentState: Works for straightforward workbooks with a clear Excel-to-SQL visualization path; not fully polished for complex sheets.
projects.futureDirections: Broader Excel edge cases, richer mapping UI, and safer production import guardrails.
deployment: Env vars / user-secrets for connection strings
features: Batch SQL apply with GO splitting

projects.title: SSMS Work — SQL Server Feature Explorer
projects.description: SQL Server feature explorer POC: minimal APIs + Dapper + Blazor Interactive Server shell covering performance, security, and replication narratives.
projects.inspiration: SQL Server Management Studio is pervasive, but I had only used tables and stored procedures—so I built a generic explorer POC to exercise other SSMS-accessible features and keep a reference codebase for future on-the-job scenarios.
projects.currentState: Features appear to work as intended; I am still building intuition for when each capability matters in real deployments.
projects.futureDirections: Deepen scenario-based docs tied to actual enterprise DBA and developer workflows.
